name: Crownfall officier shield
shortDescription: A Crownfall officer shield bearing formal marks, straight lines, and the rigid dignity of drill yards. It protects the arm, but its real work is telling nearby soldiers which orders should be obeyed first.
flavorText: “A shield can block a blow; an officer’s shield blocks confusion.”

name: buckler shield
shortDescription: A buckler shield is small, quick, and close to the hand, meant for fast work rather than grand displays. It suits duelists, scouts, and anyone who prefers defense that can keep up with a moving argument.
flavorText: “A buckler is not much wall, but it is a very loyal door.”

name: decorative buckler shield
shortDescription: A decorative buckler shield adds polish, paint, or fine bosses to a compact piece of protection. It is light enough for practice and handsome enough for ceremony, which is exactly the sort of compromise nobles enjoy buying.
flavorText: “Small shields invite decoration because there is only so much room for fear.”

name: shield of Border March brotherhood
shortDescription: The shield of the Border March brotherhood carries the stern look of frontier oaths and hard roads. Its markings speak of patrol fires, shared rations, and men who trusted the line because they trusted each other first.
flavorText: “A border shield is half wood, half promise, and half stubbornness besides.”

name: dwarven buckler shield
shortDescription: A dwarven buckler shield is compact, dense, and made with the serious confidence of people who dislike wasted metal. It feels small only until lifted, then announces that dwarven notions of portable are ambitious.
flavorText: “Dwarves make small shields the way mountains make small stones.”

name: fabled shield of Highbell
shortDescription: The fabled shield of Highbell is said to have guarded shrine steps, bell towers, and pilgrims caught between prayer and panic. Its rim seems made to hold a ringing silence, as if the next strike might sound like a warning bell.
flavorText: “Highbell’s shield rings loudest when no bell rope is near.”

name: starcourt buckler shield
shortDescription: A Starcourt buckler shield is small, polished, and patterned with the graceful restraint expected in moonlit halls. It is a duelist’s shield as much as a guard’s, quick enough for wrist work and pretty enough to be noticed afterward.
flavorText: “Starcourt even teaches a buckler to bow.”

name: fancy buckler shield
shortDescription: A fancy buckler shield turns a practical hand guard into something almost flirtatious, with trim that catches light before the metal catches danger. It sells well to people who practice in mirrors as much as courtyards.
flavorText: “A fancy buckler is for fighters who intend to win beautifully.”

name: fancy dwarven buckler shield
shortDescription: A fancy dwarven buckler shield keeps the weight and stubbornness of dwarven craft while adding enough ornament to prove the maker was showing off. The decoration is precise, deep, and probably structurally unnecessary, which makes it more impressive.
flavorText: “Dwarven luxury means the engraving is as durable as the shield.”

name: fancy starcourt buckler shield
shortDescription: A fancy Starcourt buckler shield looks like something carried to a duel where the invitation was scented. Its polished face and careful patterning make it suitable for court defense, which is to say danger with better music.
flavorText: “At Starcourt, even blocking is expected to have posture.”

name: full body shield
shortDescription: A full body shield is a walking wall, broad enough to make the bearer feel like a door with ambitions. It is heavy, awkward, and deeply reassuring when the world in front becomes unreasonable.
flavorText: “A full body shield is courage with handles.”

name: gong shield
shortDescription: A gong shield is round, resonant, and shaped to turn defense into a sound everyone remembers. One good hit can announce a fight, a warning, or the wearer’s poor timing to half a courtyard.
flavorText: “The gong shield blocks quietly only in theory.”

name: Crownfall castle shield
shortDescription: A Crownfall castle shield bears fortress colors and the stubborn geometry of battlements. It feels less like personal gear than a piece of wall assigned to one arm.
flavorText: “Crownfall shields are painted like castles because they intend to behave like them.”

name: Redvale castle shield
shortDescription: A Redvale castle shield carries red field marks, strong edges, and the severe pride of a holdfast that has seen banners change but gates remain. It is built for formation work, where individual fear is expected to hide behind shared paint.
flavorText: “Redvale paints shields like walls and expects men to stand the same way.”

name: Redvale shield
shortDescription: A Redvale shield is simpler than the castle pattern but no less direct, marked for soldiers who march under red orders and hard weather. It is dependable military stock with little patience for ornament beyond identity.
flavorText: “A Redvale shield does not ask who is watching; it faces forward.”

name: strong shield
shortDescription: A strong shield is exactly what its name promises: thick, reliable, and made for people who prefer plain virtues when danger gets close. It has no special story, which is often the reason it earns one.
flavorText: “Strong shields become famous only after they refuse to fail.”

name: shield of death
shortDescription: The shield of death is a grim piece, dark-marked and made for bearers who want their warning carried before them. Whether cursed, ceremonial, or merely theatrical, it tends to make negotiations shorter.
flavorText: “Some shields promise safety; this one promises consequence.”

name: shield of ancestry
shortDescription: The shield of ancestry bears old marks, family patterns, or worn heraldry whose meaning may outlive the family itself. It is less a tool than a portable argument that the bearer comes from somewhere worth remembering.
flavorText: “Ancestral shields protect the present by making the past stare over its shoulder.”

name: wooden shield
shortDescription: A wooden shield is cheap, repairable, and much braver than its price suggests. It smells of sap, leather, and practical desperation, which is the scent of most early adventures.
flavorText: “Wooden shields are where many heroes begin and many debts end.”

name: fabled shield of reflection
shortDescription: The fabled shield of reflection shines with a surface too clear to be ordinary metal, catching faces, light, and perhaps intent. Stories say it has turned more than glances aside, though sensible merchants charge extra for every story that might be true.
flavorText: “A reflecting shield makes the world answer for what it sends.”

name: shield of scion
shortDescription: The shield of scion is marked for inheritance, youth, and the uncomfortable moment when family expectation becomes equipment. It is polished for ceremony but balanced for real use, because heirs are most valuable when they return.
flavorText: “A scion’s shield carries two burdens: the blow and the family name.”

name: shield of snakes
shortDescription: The shield of snakes coils its design around the face like a warning that refuses to sit still. It suits poison houses, serpent cults, or theatrical duelists who understand that fear is easier to carry when painted well.
flavorText: “A snake shield does not need to hiss; the shape does enough.”

name: ironclan spiked shield
shortDescription: An Ironclan spiked shield is less a barrier than a blunt opinion about personal space. Heavy, studded, and aggressively forged, it turns defense into something that leans forward.
flavorText: “Ironclan shields do not wait politely for contact.”

name: fabled shield of Unraveling
shortDescription: The fabled shield of Unraveling is said to loosen bindings, fray hostile workings, and make complicated magic lose its grip. Its surface appears calm, but the patterns along the rim seem to untie themselves when stared at too long.
flavorText: “Some shields stop force; this one asks force what holds it together.”

name: small round shield
shortDescription: A small round shield is plain, quick, and easy to carry, made for guards, scouts, and fighters who value movement over a wall. It offers modest protection with excellent manners about not exhausting the arm.
flavorText: “A small round shield survives by being where the trouble arrives.”
